title: [ipv6_addr.c:277-279] ll_addr_out zeroed on NULL eui64 but not on other errors
description:
Round 2 gap analysis: Missed because error handling looked consistent at first glance.

In lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 lines 277-300, the ll_addr_out is zeroed on failure for the NULL eui64 case (lines 278-280), eui64_to_iid failure (lines 287-290), and make_link_local failure (lines 295-299). However, if lichen_ipv6_addr_to_str fails at lines 302-306, the function continues and returns 0 (success) without zeroing ll_addr_out.

Actually reading more carefully - line 302-306 is a LOG_WRN case where the address IS valid, just couldn't be formatted as string. The function returns success (line 314) and copies ll_addr to ll_addr_out (lines 310-312). This is correct behavior.

The code is actually correct - not a bug. Closing as false positive.
close_reason: False positive - the code correctly handles all error cases. When ipv6_addr_to_str fails, the ll_addr is still valid (it was successfully constructed), just couldn't be logged as a string. The function returns success and outputs the valid address.
